meta.source: Consolidated from Sakaguchi 2024 dissertation 『樺太アイヌ語における人称と数の標示に関する研究』 (chunks covering OCR lines 1551-3100, 3101-4650, 6201-7750, 7751-9119) + Sakaguchi 2020 『アイヌ語樺太方言における名詞複数接尾辞-ahcinの用法』; reader extractions merged 2026-06-10
meta.notation: Markers given in OUR convention ('=' for personal/postpositional clitics, '-' for derivational affixes); Sakaguchi himself hyphenates person affixes and reserves '=' for the plural clitics AHCI/AHCIN
meta.purpose: Review-gated input for reconciling the aynu-itah affix inventory (existing rows: ku= e= ci= eci= en= i= a= an= =an =as in=); nothing auto-merges
keyCorrections: in= is the 1st-person OBJECT prefix for BOTH KU (1SG) and CI (1PL) series — referent in texts always 《1SG》; the dictionary's current 'in= 1PL?' gloss is wrong (Table 7-1, diss. l.6561-6569; §3.3; Sato 1985 extended)

CI series (ci=, =as) is grammatically 1PL but in Sakhalin texts always means 《1SG》 (folktale quoted speech); corresponds to Hokkaido EXCLUSIVE 1PL, but Sakhalin has NO inclusive/exclusive distinction (fn72)

AN series (an=, =an, i=) is the 4th person (term after Nakagawa 1988): functions = indefinite person, passive agent slot, 1SG, 1PL; NOT used for 2nd-person honorific in Sakhalin (unlike Hokkaido); 2PL-honorific niche is filled by standalone utara
-(y)an in 1↔2 portmanteau circumfixes marks 2ND-PERSON PLURALITY (whether 2nd person is A or O); with 4th-person subjects the same -(y)an is just the 4.S/4.A suffix

markers.dialect: pan-Sakhalin (East coast tables; Raichishka elicitation)
markers.notes: On intransitives and transitives; transitive-A prefixes double as noun possessor (ku-seta-ha 'my dog'). Pronoun kuani. Co-occurs in one sentence with CI-series forms (diss. ex 3-3, 3-4).

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; only rarely used in West-coast North (Raichishka)
markers.notes: ci-nee 'I am' (ex 3-2). Sakaguchi REJECTS Dal Corso (2021: 84) reading ci-mahpo-hoo as exclusive-1PL; reads ci- there as 1SG possessor per 村崎 1976: 30.
markers.conflicts: Dal Corso 2021 (exclusive 1PL) vs Sakaguchi (grammatical 1PL, referent 1SG); traditional descriptions called CI '1SG' outright

markers.number: grammatically plural (triggers plural verb stem: asip-as, pay[e]-as)
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin and elsewhere
markers.notes: Never co-occurs with AHCI. Pronoun ciokay ~ cookay (cookay more frequent).

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin
markers.notes: As O with 3.A: e-reske kun kamuy 'the god who will raise you' (ex 3-26); as O with 4.A: an-e-anpa-re (ex 8-38). Pronoun eani.
markers.conflicts: 2020 art. ex 4-24 glosses e-koro as '2SG.S' though koro is transitive — gloss slip

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin
markers.notes: eci-maw-he '2PL.A-wind-POSS' possessor use (ex 3-7). Pronoun ecioka(y) ~ ecookay, used toward inferiors; honorific 2PL alternative is standalone utara. 51 tokens in 『物語』, never with AHCI (大原 1991: 54).

markers.number: singular and plural (number-neutral; animate plurality via AHCI or plural verb stem)
markers.dialect: all
markers.notes: Pronoun ani(hi) (rare: 2 tokens in 『物語』, 4 in 『資料』). 3PL pronoun okay reported by 知里 1943/1973: 547 but UNATTESTED in the texts (fn22, fn57).

markers.number: grammatically plural (attaches to plural stems: okay-an, makap-an, tup-an)
markers.referent: ①indefinite person ②passive agent slot ③《1SG》 ④《1PL》; NOT 2nd-person honorific in Sakhalin
markers.dialect: pan-Sakhalin; Tangiku (2022: 357) adds 'person in quotation' (logophoric) for West-coast North; narrative-1st-person use presumed lost in Sakhalin
markers.notes: =an has clitic-like autonomy: follows auxiliary rusuy (paye-rusuy-an; *paye-an rusuy unattested in 『物語』, fn61); always precedes AHCI (okay-an=ahsi); allomorph ay= before y/s (ay-yup-utar[i]-hi, ay-sirankore); am before m (am manu). Pronoun anoka(y), used only when 4th person = 1st person. okay-an=ahsi = explicit 《1PL》.
markers.conflicts: 村崎 1976 describes Raichishka =an as '1PL'; Sakaguchi reanalyses as 4th person. Hokkaido Saru 4th person additionally = inclusive 1PL + 2HON + quoted 1st person (田村 1988) — absent in Sakhalin. Gloss slips in diss.: okay-an=ahsi tagged 4.A at ex 5-2 but 4.S at 5-16/5-20 (4.S correct).

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin
markers.notes: With 3.A (i-ranke ex 3-10), with anokay’ahsin (ex 3-12), in imperatives (i-teere 'wait for me' ex 3-21); i-kuu-re 'gave me to drink' (Maoka, 村崎 1976: 77), i-reske 'raised me' (ex 5-5). 2020 art. glosses i= as 1SG.O or 1PL.O by context; also possessor with kinship nouns (i-hoskiram-hu, fn13).

markers.number: both: serves 1SG (KU) and 1PL (CI) — no number distinction in 1st-person O marking
markers.referent: texts: always 《1SG》 (Table 7-1); translations sometimes 「私たち」/「私と」
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; West-coast North (Raichishka) uses en= (KU) / un= (CI)
markers.notes: DICTIONARY CORRECTION TARGET (current entry 'in= 1PL?', freq 3). Sato (1985) assigned in- to CI only; Sakaguchi shows it also serves KU: ex 3-7 (imperative), 3-8 (3.A subject), 3-17 (2SG.A subject). Domain: 《単数》 meaning, quoted speech in folktales. Examples: in-raanu[h], in-raanup-an, in-etohparusi, in-kosisciwka, in-ranke, in-koyki, in-ko-ociw-rusuy yan.
markers.sources: diss. Table 3-2 l.1663-1681, §3.3.1.1 l.1830-1895, §3.3.2.1 l.2057-2110, Table 7-1 l.6561-6569; Sato, T. (1985) 'The First Person Objective Affix in- in the East Coast Dialects of Sakhalin Ainu' (diss. bibliography l.8657-8660)
markers.conflicts: Sato 1985 (CI-only) vs Sakaguchi 2024 (KU and CI); ex 5-12 gloss '1.PL.O' vs Table 7-1 referent 《1SG》

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; Raichishka = en=
markers.notes: in-etohparusi co-occurring with ku= context (ex 3-17), in-koyki with cookay (ex 3-19). *e-in- and bare *i- NOT used for 2SG.A>1SG.O; Kindaichi's e-i(n)- unattested in texts.

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; Raichishka: en-…-yan (KU), un-…-yan (CI) (服部 1961: 16)
markers.notes: in-raanup-an (exx 3-22, 3-23). Piłsudski (1912: 92) misanalysed as variant of inranu; Sakaguchi: difference of subject person. Possibly underlying -yan with y lost after consonant (fn47). Distinguish imperative in-ranke an = in= + particle (y)an (ex 3-24).
markers.conflicts: Piłsudski's own analysis corrected by Sakaguchi

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; Raichishka uses bare i- (服部 1961: 16; 村崎 1976: 50, fn31)
markers.notes: Regular composition; co-occurs with an= (ex 3-18) and anoka (ex 3-20); also e-i-rayki '2SG.A.4.O' (ex 8-37). Also 金田一 1960: 129; Novikova & Savel'eva 2000/1967: 314.

markers.number: A sg or pl; O singular
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; same in Saru, Chitose, Raichishka; *ku-e-, *ci-e- unattested anywhere in Ainu
markers.notes: eci-makan-ke-he, eci-sam-te, eci-ko-ruura (ku= contexts, exx 3-27/3-28); eci-konte with kuani (ex 3-29); eci-nukan-te with =as (exx 3-30, 7-10, 8-39); eci-koyki with cookay+eani (ex 3-31). Homophonous with plain 2PL eci= — disambiguated by context.

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin
markers.notes: Regular composition; with anoka (exx 3-32, 3-33, 8-38) and anokay’ahsin (ex 3-35): an-e-anpa-re, an-e-makan-ke, an-e-nukara=hsi.
markers.conflicts: 金田一 (1960: 128) wrongly generalizes an-e- to ALL 1>2SG

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; same in Raichishka (服部 1961: 14; 村崎 1976: 50)
markers.notes: eci-ko-aa-re-yan (prayer, ex 3-37), eci-ko-urenka-re-yan (ex 3-38); plain eci- once for 1>2PL (ex 3-39, cookay treated as common noun).

markers.role: 4.A 《1SG/1PL》 > 2PL.O — here -(y)an is simply the 4.A subject suffix
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin; same in Raichishka
markers.notes: eci-nukara-yan (ex 3-40), eci-sam-te-yan (exx 3-41, 8-41), eci-ama-rusuy-yan (after auxiliary, ex 3-43), eci-rayke-an (ex 3-44), eci-ruura-an (ex 3-45), ec[i]-eh-te-yan (ex 8-22). In ditransitives indexes A + INDIRECT object: eci-rayke-re-yan (ex 3-46, 8-40).
markers.conflicts: Table 8-1 readings: -yan = 《1複》 in ex 8-40 but 《1単》 in ex 8-41 (semantic, not formal, difference)

markers.dialect: East Sakhalin (1 token only: 『物語』 16306-07); Raichishka: i-…-yan, and eci-i- in elders' speech (村崎 1979: 50)
markers.notes: ex 3-25 eci-i-nukara. Kindaichi's eci-i(n)-/eci-in- unattested. 2PL.A > 4.O 《1PL》 unattested.

markers.role: O of passive-like construction; unanalysable unit (not in-ci-)
markers.number: no sg/pl distinction (serves KU and CI)
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin (2 tokens); corresponds to NE-Hokkaido enci- (sg) / unci- (pl) (美幌, 三石, 帯広, 本別, 石狩, 釧路, 静内)
markers.notes: inci-wenkara with cookay (ex 3-13 = Sato 1985: 159 ex 9), inci-tuye in KU context (ex 3-14). Agent is definite, so not indefinite-person construction; agent can be oblique with orowa (Hokkaido parallel ex 3-16).

markers.dialect: West-coast North (Uschs/鵜城 texts, 北原編 2013: 10, 18); East-coast table cell
markers.notes: fn50: Dal Corso (2021: 84) lists bare an- for this; Sakaguchi doubts — should be an-i-.

markers.role: imperative final particle for plural addressees (possibly polite imperative)
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin (+ general)
markers.notes: Requires plural stem of number-distinguishing intransitives; follows even transitive verbs (unlike 4th-person =an); can be followed by conjunctions te, kaane (ex 3-21); occupies same slot as AHCI (ex 5-12); singular-addressee imperative = bare singular stem, plural-addressee = plural stem + yan (l.7529-7532); exception eci-eraman! (『物語』 09212, fn144). Historically related to 2PL suffix -(y)an (fn35; 田村 1988: 61); possibly related to causative allomorph -yara (fn33). 村崎 1979: 30 treated V-yan as object-plural forms.
markers.conflicts: fn75: may be better treated as a kind of person affix; Murasaki's object-plural analysis superseded

markers.role: marks plurality of subject OR of human PRIMARY object (monotransitive patient / ditransitive indirect object) by modifying person inflection (incl. zero 3rd person)
markers.person: attaches to 3rd- and 4th-person (and possessed) finite forms; never with 1↔2 portmanteau marking; no co-occurrence with CI-series or eci= 2PL
markers.dialect: spelling =ahsi/=hsi/=si in katakana sources (『物語』), =ahci/=hci/=ci in Piłsudski transcriptions; =ahte Shiranushi (知里 1975/1954: 505); -(a)hsin southern per Piłsudski 1912: 36
markers.notes: Optional (selective), unlike person affixes. Attaches AFTER =an (okay-an=ahsi, tup-an=ahci), BEFORE nominalizer -ihi/-hV (u-e-ramuokay=ahsi-hi). Allomorphy (知里 1973/1942: 498): short V + =hci; long V + =ci or V shortens + hci (neesi ~ nehci); C + =asi/=ahsi. Underlying stem-final p/t/k resurfaces: tup=ahci, makap=ahsi, rikip=asi (cf. rikin), usap=ahte. NOT on nominalized verbs (丹菊 2015: 24) — dummy kii carries it (an-kii=si; 25/29 kii=AHCI tokens). Not in relative clause whose head is utara/utah. Not used when 1st/2nd person affixes already encode number. Maoka: -an=sin → /-aysin/ (n→y before s, ex 5-1). First noted by Dobrotvorskij (as past-tense particle), identified as plural by Piłsudski (1912: 32, 37); 金田一 1913: 32, 53 called it 「語勢」/二人以上ノ動作. Historically related to Hokkaido ci (Nakagawa & Fukazawa 2022: 275-276).
markers.conflicts: 服部 1961: 'obligatory for 3PL nominative' vs Sakaguchi: optional, also marks primary-object plurality vs Dal Corso 2021: S/A accusative-aligned animate agreement. East coast has less prominent 3PL verb marking than West (Bugaeva 2016: 96, fn73).

markers.role: plural of nouns — ONLY on possessed (personal) forms and nominalizers; additive (homogeneous) plural only; never on proper nouns or conceptual forms
markers.person: attaches outside possessive -hV: an-seta-ha-hcin, ku=micihi=hcin, an-mahpoo-ho=sin, utari-hi-hcin
markers.dialect: -(a)hsin southern (Piłsudski); -sin the only shape in 山邊・金田一 1913; a is fixed (*-uhcin, *-ehcin do not occur)
markers.notes: Murasaki 2013 calls it 'possessive plural'. Distinct morpheme from verbal AHCI per Sakaguchi (2020 fn2; contra 知里, 丹菊 who unify them). Without =ahcin the possessed form is strictly singular (incl. distributive singular: an-seta-ha 'our (shared or each) one dog'; 服部 1961: 7 — obligatory plural marking; 知里 1942/1944 treated it as optional, fn16). On nominalizers it pluralizes the clause-internal event participants (siri-hcin, humhi-śin, hawe-sin, hawʼoroke-sin, rúhe-śin); not added if the clause verb already bears plural suffix. Not added when -utara or numeral 'two' already present in the NP. With pronoun: anokay-(y)ahcin, eciokayahcin. Contact-induced extension from verbal domain via Uilta/Nivkh suspected (diss. ch.6 summary l.8398-8406).
markers.conflicts: ahci vs ahcin same-or-different morpheme: Sakaguchi separate; 知里/丹菊 same. 村崎 1979 claim '-utara does not attach to possessed forms' shown wrong (an-cinkew-he utara).

markers.role: plural of nouns — unrestricted: conceptual forms, possessed forms, proper nouns; additive AND associative plural (and exemplary-associative with personal names)
markers.dialect: West-coast variants uta, utah; possessed -utari(hi); -utarike = -utari + -ke '~の所' (2020 fn45)
markers.notes: Eekichi-utara 'Eekichi and those with him' (associative); Muramatsu Yoshino -utara = exemplary-associative (only the two named; 山越 2001: 268 criterion); tehpo utara 'guns and such' (inanimate +α, ex 8-19). Not added to nominalizers. Distinct from free noun utara 'kin, companions' and from standalone quasi-pronoun utara.

markers.role: S/A-slot quasi-clitic noun (between NEG ham/hane and verb — same slot as person prefixes); a few O tokens (O utara V order usual, 17/28 in 『資料』)
markers.person: anaphoric 3PL 'they'; generic 'people'; self-reference 1PL (incl. hearer); honorific address 2PL
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin texts; West-coast uta(h)
markers.notes: Verb shows NO AHCI agreement when standalone utara is subject (佐藤 1987). Self-reference use looks INCLUSIVE (exx 4-22/4-23); exclusive contexts use anokay(ahcin) (ex 4-24). Derived from formal noun -utara with elided modifier. Fills the 2PL-honorific niche (4th person not used for 2HON in Sakhalin).

markers.role: adverb-like position, not syntactic A/S/O arguments (知里 1973/1942: 547); optional; a pronoun used as oblique blocks object marking on the verb (ex 3-47 kuani e-eramuskari, no in=)
markers.person: kuani 1SG (KU) / ciokay~cookay 1PL-form 《1SG》 (CI) / eani 2SG / ecioka(y)~ecookay 2PL / ani(hi) 3SG / anoka(y) 4 (only when = 1st person) / anokay-(y)ahcin ~ anokay’ahsin dedicated 1PL
markers.dialect: anokayahcin: 1PL for ages 20-30+, but 1SG for men 60+ in Raichishka (服部 1961: 6); 『物語』 anokayʼa(h)sin ×102 vs anokayʼutara ×14; Piłsudski anoka(y) ×64
markers.notes: anihi also '~self' (村崎 1979: 78) and once 2SG-honorific address to husband (ex 4-21) — parallels Hokkaido anokay spouse-address (Teshio, Bihoro), a use absent in Sakhalin where anihi covers it (fn20, fn67). A and O may be indexed on different stems in one clause (ex 3-48).

markers.role: 1PL (CI: =as S, ci= A, in= O) and 4 (AN: =an S, an= A, i= O): TRIPARTITE; 2SG, 2PL, 3: NEUTRAL; 1SG (KU: ku= A/S, in= O): NOMINATIVE-ACCUSATIVE; 4 could be nom-acc if =an and an= identified (fn25)
markers.dialect: East Sakhalin
markers.notes: Object affixes index the RECIPIENT (primary object) of ditransitives, not the theme — secondary-object language (Haspelmath 2005: 427-429); max two arguments indexed (subject + primary object); ditransitive themes never person-indexed, hence never take AHCI (ex 8-41). Sato's (1985) class-agreement rule: i= with AN class, in= with CI class — Sakaguchi extends in= to KU. KU and CI distinguished ONLY in pronouns + nominative affixes.

markers.role: number-distinguishing intransitives agree with the GRAMMATICAL number of the person affix, not the referent count: =as/=an take plural stems (asip-as, makap-an, okay-an) even for 1SG referents; when =an is dropped the stem matches referent number (makan, ex 7-7)
markers.notes: 3rd person alternates by construal: continuous group = SG stem, individuated = PL stem; numeral-quantified and poronno-quantified subjects tend to SG stem (poroono an ×35 vs poronno okay ×1, okay=ahsi ×2) but not absolute. Transitive SG/PL pairs (mesu/mespa etc.) track the patient; 'PL' often = iterative/distributed action.
markers.conflicts: gloss slip: e-oman tagged 行く.PL at ex 7-11 (oman is SG member of oman/paye)

conflictsSummary: in= number/series: dictionary 'in= 1PL?' → correct to 1.O of BOTH series (Sato 1985 CI-only, extended to KU by Sakaguchi 2024); West-coast North uses en=/un= instead
CI series: grammatically 1PL, referentially 1SG; Dal Corso's exclusive-1PL reading of ci- rejected by Sakaguchi; Sakhalin lacks inclusive/exclusive entirely (fn72)
an=/=an: 村崎's Raichishka '1PL' vs Sakaguchi's 4th person; Hokkaido 4th-person functions (incl-1PL, 2HON, narrative-1SG) reduced in Sakhalin to indefinite/passive-agent/1SG/1PL
4>4 passive: an-i- (Sakaguchi) vs bare an- (Dal Corso 2021: 84)
AHCI function: 3PL-subject agreement (服部/Dal Corso framings) vs subject-or-primary-object plurality clitic (Sakaguchi); obligatoriness disputed
AHCI vs AHCIN: one morpheme (知里, 丹菊) vs two (Sakaguchi 2020/2024)
anokayahcin reference: 1PL generally, but 1SG for Raichishka men 60+ (服部 1961: 6)
okay 3PL pronoun (知里 1943) unattested in texts

Internal gloss slips flagged by readers: okay-an=ahsi as 4.A (ex 5-2, should be 4.S); e-oman 行く.PL (ex 7-11); e-koro 2SG.S (2020 ex 4-24); eaykah/easkay glosses apparently swapped (diss. l.4234-4238 citing 服部 1961) — verify in PDF
Piłsudski 1912: 92 analysed inranupan as 'instead of inranu' (in 'me' + ranu 'to love'); Sakaguchi corrects: in-raanup-an, lemma *raanup
